mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes operator that provides declarative APIs and tooling to simplify the provisioning, upgrading, and management of multiple Kubernetes clusters. It is particularly useful for platform operators who need to manage clusters across various infrastructure environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

```

> This is the official repository for Cluster API Helm charts.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Cluster API to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

```

> This YAML file contains all necessary CRDs.

steps.title: Install Cluster API components
steps.description:
This step installs the Cluster API components using Helm, which will manage the lifecycle of your clusters.

```bash

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.4

```

> Ensure you are installing in the kube-system namespace.

steps.title: Create a Cluster API management cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a management cluster that will be used to manage other clusters.

```bash

clusterctl init --infrastructure aws

```

> Replace 'aws' with your desired infrastructure provider.

steps.title: Verify Cluster API installation
steps.description:
This step checks that the Cluster API components are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> You should see the Cluster API components running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cluster API components
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Cluster API components and CRDs. Be aware that this will lead to data loss for any clusters managed by Cluster API.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace kube-system

kubectl delete -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

```

> This removes the CRDs.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API components
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your clusters. This step will upgrade the Cluster API components to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -o json > clusters-backup.json

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.4

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> Backup your current cluster state.
> Upgrade to the specified version.
> Verify that the pods are running the new version.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Cluster" in version "cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Cluster CRD is not installed.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml' to install the CRDs.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster: unable to create control plane'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the infrastructure provider is not correctly configured.

**Fix:**
Check your infrastructure provider configuration and ensure all required credentials are set.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'unable to connect to the server: dial tcp <IP>:<PORT>: connect: connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the API server is not reachable.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the API server is running and that your kubeconfig is correctly configured.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no resources found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Cluster API components are not deployed.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl get pods -n kube-system' to check the status of the components.

versionNotes.changes: Improved support for AWS and Azure infrastructure providers, added new features for cluster scaling.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Kubernetes versions below 1.26.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.11.x, ensure to update your infrastructure provider configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see the Cluster API components running with 'kubectl get pods -n kube-system' showing all pods in 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required tools installed.
